next_steps: Obtain serum vitamin A, retinol-binding protein, and zinc levels
Obtain serum vitamin B12, folate, copper, and ceruloplasmin levels
Initiate emergent vitamin A supplementation (oral or parenteral)
Consult pediatric ophthalmology and clinical nutrition
Ocular surface lubrication and aggressive monitoring of corneal integrity
Dietary counseling and evaluation for nutritional rehabilitation
Obtain serum retinol, retinol-binding protein, and zinc levels
Obtain serum levels of Vitamin B12, folate, copper, ceruloplasmin, and Vitamin C
Initiate urgent vitamin A supplementation (oral or parenteral per WHO protocols)
Begin ocular surface lubrication and aggressive monitoring for corneal ulceration/perforation
Consult pediatric ophthalmology, clinical nutrition, and pediatric psychiatry for ARFID management
Screen for additional systemic micronutrient deficiencies (Vitamin D, E, K, iron studies)
Perform serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D and calcium level check to investigate calvarial findings
